rationale: In the hypothesis-development section, the citation is tied to a core theoretical premise about justification in hedonic versus utilitarian choice, not merely listed as broad background. The citing paper relies on this premise to explain why utilitarian-seeming cues (more attributes) can disproportionately help hedonic options. Although the citation appears in a grouped parenthetical and is not methodologically reused, it meaningfully informs the conceptual framing of the mechanism.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Kivetz and Simonson (2002) as conceptual support for the claim that hedonic options are harder to justify than utilitarian ones. That justification logic helps motivate the citing paper's mechanism that adding attributes increases perceived usefulness and shifts choice toward hedonic options.
